title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning as the Red Sox lost 4-3 to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, giving up no runs and striking out five over seven innings.
The Red Sox have lost six of their last seven games and are 23-33, 10 games under .500.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics fans and the TD Garden atmosphere during a recent livestream.
Boston Celtics are linked to trade rumors involving Antetokounmpo, but Miami Heat are favored to land him.
Speculation includes whether Jaylen Brown could be part of a trade to acquire Antetokounmpo for the Celtics.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police dispersed the annual Harvard Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge, citing safety concerns over crowd size.
Authorities responded to reports of graduates blocking the bridge and attempting to swim in the Charles River.
The event followed Harvard's Class of 2026 commencement, where Conan O'Brien delivered the keynote address.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' featuring Uma Thurman is now streaming on Peacock.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2 with Tina Fey and Steve Carell is available on Netflix.
'Spider-Noir' starring Nicolas Cage as a 1930s Spider-Man streams on Prime Video.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: ESPN's Kiley McDaniel predicts Red Sox will draft 18-year-old third baseman Bo Lowrance in July's MLB Draft.
Bo Lowrance, 6-foot-5, from South Carolina, is projected first-round at No. 20; committed to University of Virginia.
Lowrance praised for hitting ability; scouts compare him to Freddie Freeman but suggest potential switch to first base.

title: Lowell father and daughter charged in alleged payroll tax scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lim Ou, 69, and Nalen Ou, 39, of Lowell, charged with conspiracy and failure to pay payroll taxes.
Prosecutors allege over $48 million was concealed in cash payments to workers via Worcester check-cashing.
Charges could result in up to five years in prison and $250,000 fines, with federal court appearance pending.

title: Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez is absent from Patriots OTAs due to contract extension talks.
Gonzalez seeks a significant pay raise from his current $4 million rookie contract.
Patriots have cap flexibility after restructuring Mike Onwenu's contract.

title: Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A suspect in a drug investigation led police on a chase through Everett, Malden, and Stoneham before escaping.
The driver struck a State Police cruiser and nearly hit a Malden officer before abandoning the car in Stoneham.
The suspect, with several outstanding warrants, is being actively searched for by police after fleeing on foot.

title: Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jerod Mayo, ex-Patriots head coach, is now managing director at Fifth Down Capital, a private equity firm.
Mayo served as head coach of the Patriots during the 2024 season, finishing with a 4-13 record.
Fifth Down Capital, based in Needham, invests in technology, healthcare, and consumer/retail sectors.

title: Mass. man reports beating Mount Everest speed climbing record
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Andrews from Massachusetts climbed Mount Everest in 9 hours and 55 minutes, beating the previous 2003 record.
Nepali climber Lhakpa Gelu Sherpa held the previous speed record of 10 hours and 56 minutes for summiting Everest.
Andrews required seven attempts to set the new record, overcoming challenges such as weather and gear malfunctions.
